channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australia Doubles Graduate Visa Fees and Introduces Other Changes
item.description: Australia has announced a significant increase in application fees for the 485 graduate visa, a visa that allows international students to stay and work in Australia for two years post-graduation. Fro...

item.title: Building Sustainable Habits: How Connecting with Your Values Makes Change Last
item.description: In this article, Dr. Jennifer Caspari explains why relying on willpower alone is often insufficient for building lasting habits. Instead of focusing on self-control, Caspari advocates for connecting n...

item.title: PDEA Declares Cotabato City’s LGU Operations Center Drug-Free
item.description: On March 2, 2026, the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA) officially declared the People’s Palace in Cotabato City as a drug-free workplace. The declaration was made during a formal ceremony hel...

item.title: House Committee Approves Anti-Political Dynasty Bill, Sees It as Major Step in Political Reform
item.description: The House Committee on Suffrage and Electoral Reforms, led by Rep. Zia Alonto Adiong, has approved a substitute bill aimed at addressing the issue of political dynasties in the Philippines. Adiong des...

item.title: How the Iran Conflict is Affecting UK Energy Prices and Consumer Bills
item.description: The ongoing Iran conflict is having significant economic ramifications that are already pushing up global oil and gas prices. The Strait of Hormuz, a key oil trade route, has been affected by Iranian ...

item.title: Iranian Canadians in Regina Consider Impacts of Escalating Middle East Tensions

item.title: Niger and France's Growing Tensions: What’s Behind the Conflict?
item.description: Tensions between Niger and France have significantly increased following the attack on Diori Hamani International Airport and Air Base in Niamey. Niger's government has publicly accused France of bein...

item.title: Journalist's Interview with Ian Huntley Sparks Police Suspicion After Girls' Disappearance

item.title: Reporting From Iran Amid Internet Blackouts: How Journalists Are Adapting
item.description: After Israeli and American strikes killed senior Iranian officials in Tehran, the Iranian government imposed a near-total internet blackout. This move has severely impacted journalists and activists i...

item.title: Angus Taylor promises to reset relationship between Liberals and big business

item.title: Superboy Cheque Opens Up About Struggles with Acne and Self-Esteem

item.title: IPOA Investigates Death of 14-Year-Old Boy in Kisauni Shooting Incident
item.description: The Independent Policing Oversight Authority (IPOA) has launched an investigation into the fatal shooting of 14-year-old Dennis Ringa Swaleh in Kisauni, Mombasa County. The teenager was shot on New Ye...

item.title: Muntinlupa opens interactive road safety park for children
item.description: Muntinlupa has inaugurated the Philippines' first-ever road safety park, designed to educate children about road safety in a fun, interactive way. The park, located in Barangay Tunasan, spans 3,140 sq...

item.title: Assessing the Quality of Child Care: What Parents Need to Know
item.description: Quality early childhood care plays a crucial role in the developmental foundation of children. In Canada and around the world, early learning and care programs have seen increased government investmen...

item.title: Dubai International Airport Welcomes 3.2 Million Pakistani Passengers in 2025, Hits New Record
item.description: Dubai International Airport (DXB) is set to welcome 3.2 million passengers from Pakistan in 2025, solidifying its position as a key transit hub for South Asian travellers. In the first nine months of ...

item.title: PM's Internship Scheme Faces Challenges in Providing Adequate Corporate Exposure to Youth
item.description: The Prime Minister's Internship Scheme (PMIS), launched with the goal of offering young job-seekers exposure to the corporate world, has experienced a less-than-ideal start. The programme, designed to...

item.title: Rare Ferrari Collection Discovered in Essex Warehouse Following Mechanic’s Passing
item.description: A remarkable collection of classic Ferrari cars, worth an estimated £600,000, has been discovered inside a sealed warehouse in the Essex countryside. The collection, belonging to the late Eddie Walsh,...

item.title: New Oil Storage Facility at Dar Port to End Fuel Shortages
item.description: The Oil Receiving and Storage Tanks Construction Project at Dar es Salaam's Port in Kigamboni is progressing well, aiming to address fuel shortages in Tanzania. Once completed, the facility will signi...
